description:
secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:231-258 and :285-308 contain two near-identical 28-line closures that classify SDK errors into SecretError variants. They share the same throttling-code list, the same NotFound mapping, and the same fall-through to Backend.

Maintenance consequence: when classification rules change (as they did in commit 96cd4c7 for sister crates), both sites must be updated in lockstep. Finding F1 (KmsInternalException/DependencyTimeoutException not handled) is exactly this drift in action — the rule was updated for SecretsManager but missed in two places here.

Sister crate secretx-aws-sm/src/lib.rs:91-120 already extracts a fn map_get_error helper. KMS should do the same, generic over <E> with is_not_found_exception() either via a trait bound or a small enum dispatch. Simpler: two top-level fn map_sign_error and fn map_get_public_key_error helpers.
title: Error mapping logic duplicated between sign() and public_key_der()
